prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye be askin' why the heavens above be colored like the finest sapphires in all the seven seas? Well, hoist yer ears and listen well, for I'll tell ye a truth known to any salt-crusted sea dog who's spent his days squintin' at the horizon!

The great blue canopy above our heads be that way on account o' the air itself! Ye see, when the sun's rays come blazin' down from above, they be crashin' into all manner o' tiny bits floatin' in the air like invisible powder. These wee particles be fond o' the blue part o' the light, scatterin' it about in all directions, while lettin' the other colors sail straight through!

'Tis why when ye gaze up from the deck o' yer ship, blue fills yer vision like the endless ocean herself. And mark me words, when the sun be sinkin' low, castin' its final glow 'cross the waters, them reds and oranges finally get their chance to shine, as the blue light gets lost in the vast expanse o' sky it must travel through.

Any buccaneer worth his salt knows the sky and sea be our constant companions, both wearin' their coats o' blue as if they was cut from the same cloth by Davy Jones himself!
